title: [1.19] GCP Storage Bucket binding - enabling intra-bucket copy/move between key prefixes.
body:
## Describe the feature

Today the GCP Storage Bucket binding's `copy` and `move` operations only support **cross-bucket, same-key** transfers — the destination key is hardcoded to the source's key, and `destinationBucket` is required. See [`bindings/gcp/bucket/bucket.go`](https://github.com/dapr/components-contrib/blob/main/bindings/gcp/bucket/bucket.go).

This blocks copying/moving an object between key prefixes in the **same bucket** (e.g. `public/file.xlsx` → `adhoc/file.xlsx`). GCS has no folders and the existing mechanism would is an object-key rewrite. The only workaround today is `get` + `create`, which streams the bytes through the app and sidecar and drops content-type, custom metadata, and ACLs.

### Proposal

1. Add an optional `destinationKey` to the `copy`/`move` `data` payload (defaults to source `key`).
2. Make `destinationBucket` optional (defaults to the component's configured bucket).
3. Reject only the case where **both** are omitted.

Example (the new same-bucket case):

```json
{
  "operation": "copy",
  "metadata": { "key": "public/file.xlsx" },
  "data":     { "destinationKey": "adhoc/file.xlsx" }
}
```

## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** GCP Storage Bucket binding `copy`/`move` now accept an optional `destinationKey` and treat `destinationBucket` as optional, enabling intra-bucket copy/move between key prefixes.
